setting.note: The West Coast of the South Island, Te Tai Poutini: a narrow strip of black-sand beaches, tidal lagoons and rainforest pinned between the Southern Alps and the Tasman Sea. Kerewin Holmes lives alone in a tower she built on the coast near the Okarito lagoon; the mute child Simon and the Maori man Joe come into her solitude. The map runs the wet, wild coast from Okarito north to the old gold port of Hokitika, with one journey east across the island to Christchurch. The novel is steeped in Maori language and Kai Tahu presence, and greenstone, pounamu, runs through it.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Keri Hulme, first published 1984. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Bone People, Keri Hulme (1984). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of pounamu, the West Coast greenstone that runs through the novel. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
